meta.description: Golden tests for realized variance estimators (Parkinson, Garman-Klass, Rogers-Satchell, Yang-Zhang)

reference_source.name: Academic Papers
reference_source.vendor: Journal of Business / Annals of Applied Probability

extra.parkinson_reference: Parkinson, M. (1980). 'The Extreme Value Method for Estimating the Variance of the Rate of Return.' Journal of Business, 53(1), 61-65.
extra.garman_klass_reference: Garman, M. B., & Klass, M. J. (1980). 'On the Estimation of Security Price Volatilities from Historical Data.' Journal of Business, 53(1), 67-78.
extra.rogers_satchell_reference: Rogers, L. C. G., & Satchell, S. E. (1991). 'Estimating Variance From High, Low and Closing Prices.' Annals of Applied Probability, 1(4), 504-512.
extra.yang_zhang_reference: Yang, D., & Zhang, Q. (2000). 'Drift-Independent Volatility Estimation Based on High, Low, Open, and Close Prices.' Journal of Business, 73(3), 477-491.

case_meta.notes: Two-day data with H/L ratios of 1.1 and 1.2. Formula: σ² = [1/(4·ln(2))] · (1/n) · Σ[ln(H/L)]²

annualized_variance.notes: Hand-calculated: ln(1.1)=0.0953, ln(1.2)=0.1823, sum_sq=0.0423, factor=0.3607, daily=0.00764, annual=1.924
Hand-calculated: Day1 contrib=0.00982, Day2 contrib=0.00893, daily=0.009375, annual=2.362

case_meta.notes: Two-day data. Formula: σ² = (1/n) · Σ[ln(H/C)·ln(H/O) + ln(L/C)·ln(L/O)]. Drift-independent estimator.

annualized_variance.notes: Hand-calculated: Day1: hc*ho+lc*lo=0.009567; Day2: 0.008694; daily=0.009131; annual=2.3009

case_meta.notes: Four-day data (minimum for stable YZ estimate). Combines overnight, open-close, and Rogers-Satchell components.

annualized_variance.notes: Yang-Zhang (2000) original paper uses n divisor for RS component
